core_lessons.what_this_is: The recurring counter-industry-norm ideas Amir teaches over and over to position himself as unique AND bust the myths keeping prospects from buying. These are the 'big ideas' that fuel content — each piece of content should deliver ONE of these as its paradigm shift / 'aha' moment. Source: Amir's 'Email Mimic Prompts' guide, 'Your Core Lessons' section.
core_lessons.how_each_lesson_works: Every core lesson does one (usually both) of two jobs: (1) Establish Amir's unique position in the market, and/or (2) Bust a myth/wrong belief that's holding the prospect back from buying.
core_lessons.how_to_use: When writing paradigm-shift content (emails, Problem Awareness / Turning Point / Value Share / Self-Selection / FAObjection posts), anchor the piece on ONE core lesson. Teach the 'what' and 'why' of the lesson; sell the 'how' (the offer). Don't force a lesson onto pure plug-and-play lists or behind-the-scenes content.

lessons.lesson: "Good enough" copy isn't good enough anymore
lessons.industry_norm_it_counters: AI lets you pump out 'good enough' content at scale, and volume wins
lessons.the_myth_or_why_norm_is_wrong: In a sea of AI-generated robocontent, 'good enough' is invisible. The bar didn't drop — it rose. People are starving for human connection, and mediocre copy won't cut it much longer.
lessons.the_aha: The flood of generic AI content is the opportunity. When everyone sounds the same, sounding like a human is the unfair advantage.
lessons.deeper_truth: Sameness is the enemy. Distinctiveness is the moat.
lessons.audience_belief_it_shifts: AI content is going to get flagged/ignored — sameness problem
lessons.ladders_to_offer: A voice-matched system that makes you the human in the feed of robots
one_liners: Everyone's using the same AI playbook. That's exactly why it's not working anymore.
'Good enough' is the most expensive content you can publish. It costs you the click.
In a feed full of robots, sounding human is a superpower.

lessons.lesson: ChatGPT learns by example, not by explanation
lessons.industry_norm_it_counters: Write better prompts. Add more adjectives, modifiers, and instructions to get your voice.
lessons.the_myth_or_why_norm_is_wrong: All the 'explaining' in the world — every adjective and guideline — won't make AI write in your voice. It learns best by example. Mimic prompts (feeding it your actual writing) do what instructions can't.
lessons.the_aha: Stop describing your voice to the AI. Show it. Paste your real writing and let it study you.
lessons.deeper_truth: Context (examples of YOU) beats instructions every time. Mimic prompts > instruction prompts.
lessons.audience_belief_it_shifts: More prompts = better results / AI can't capture my voice
lessons.ladders_to_offer: The mimic-prompt method and voice-DNA system
one_liners: You can't adjective your way to your own voice. Show it, don't describe it.
AI doesn't learn from your instructions. It learns from your examples.
Stop collecting prompts. Start feeding it YOU.

lessons.lesson: Most AI copywriting advice misses the point completely
lessons.industry_norm_it_counters: The endless 'steal my 500 ChatGPT prompts' / prompt-pack content everywhere online
lessons.the_myth_or_why_norm_is_wrong: Most of it is absolute poop. It's all about clever prompts and tricks, and it skips the one thing that actually matters: feeding the AI your voice and context.
lessons.the_aha: The reason your AI content sucks isn't that you have the wrong prompts. It's that everyone taught you to focus on the wrong thing.
lessons.deeper_truth: Prompt engineering is a distraction. Context engineering is the game.
lessons.audience_belief_it_shifts: I've tried prompts/courses/tools and they didn't work
lessons.ladders_to_offer: Amir's approach as the contrarian, correct alternative
one_liners: 99% of AI copywriting advice is teaching you to polish the wrong thing.
The prompt-pack industry sold you tricks. You needed a system.
If better prompts were the answer, you'd have your voice by now.

lessons.lesson: AI can be MORE creative than you, not less
lessons.industry_norm_it_counters: AI kills creativity / AI can only produce generic, soulless work
lessons.the_myth_or_why_norm_is_wrong: Creativity is making connections between different patterns and ideas — and that's exactly what AI is great at. Used right, it's a creativity amplifier, not a creativity killer.
lessons.the_aha: AI isn't the enemy of your creativity. With the right inputs, it's the fastest brainstorm partner you've ever had.
lessons.deeper_truth: Creativity = connecting patterns. That's a strength of AI, not a weakness.
one_liners: AI doesn't kill creativity. Bad inputs do.
Creativity is just connecting dots. Guess what's great at connecting dots?
The blank page problem dies the day you learn to use AI right.

lessons.lesson: Secondary rewards are what make writing fun to read
lessons.industry_norm_it_counters: Just deliver clear information / value, and the writing will work
lessons.the_myth_or_why_norm_is_wrong: Information alone is boring. Idioms, metaphors, personal stories, rhythm, alliteration — the 'secondary rewards' — are what make content read well and actually entertain your audience.
lessons.the_aha: People don't keep reading because your info is good. They keep reading because it's fun.
lessons.deeper_truth: Entertainment is the vehicle for persuasion. Never be boring — it's the only real sin.
lessons.ladders_to_offer: The 'secondary rewards' editing prompt and T.A.L.E. method
one_liners: Nobody ever bought because your tutorial was thorough.
Boring is the only unforgivable sin in copy.
Information informs. Rhythm and story persuade.

lessons.lesson: Most multi-thousand-dollar courses are overpriced fluff
lessons.industry_norm_it_counters: Premium = expensive. The $5,000 mastermind / $300-a-month membership is how you learn this.
lessons.the_myth_or_why_norm_is_wrong: Most are stuffed with fluff to justify the price, and designed around the coach's business interests, not yours. Learning AI copywriting is actually simple — there's no need to over-complicate it.
lessons.the_aha: The high price tag isn't a signal of quality. It's often a signal of padding.
lessons.deeper_truth: Simplicity wins. Most education is overpriced fluff dressed up to look hard.
lessons.audience_belief_it_shifts: $997 is a lot / I need an expensive program to learn this
lessons.ladders_to_offer: Amir's accessible, no-fluff coaching positioned against $15K masterminds
one_liners: If it takes 40 hours of 'modules' to teach, it's padded on purpose.
You're not paying for the knowledge. You're paying for the fluff that hides how simple it is.
Big price tags hide small ideas.

lessons.lesson: You learn copywriting by DOING, not by consuming
lessons.industry_norm_it_counters: Buy the course, watch the calls, consume the modules and you'll learn copy
lessons.the_myth_or_why_norm_is_wrong: You don't learn to write by listening to someone talk about writing. You learn by writing, getting feedback, and doing it again. Email coaching (do homework, get feedback) beats info-dump courses.
lessons.the_aha: Every course you've bought failed you for the same reason: it gave you information when you needed reps.
lessons.deeper_truth: Skill comes from practice + feedback, not from consumption.
lessons.audience_belief_it_shifts: I need more information / another course to get good
one_liners: You don't learn to box by watching boxing.
Information isn't the bottleneck. Reps and feedback are.
Another course won't fix what only practice can.

lessons.lesson: Talk to AI like a person, not like you're writing code
lessons.industry_norm_it_counters: Prompting is a technical skill — learn the syntax, the formulas, the 'prompt engineering'
lessons.the_myth_or_why_norm_is_wrong: The best way to get what you want from AI is to talk to it like an assistant — a person. Positive reinforcement ('Good job!', 'I trust you') genuinely gets better results. It's not code.
lessons.the_aha: You don't need to learn a programming language. You already know how to brief an assistant — just do that.
lessons.deeper_truth: AI responds to human communication, including encouragement. Treat it like a smart intern, not a compiler.
lessons.audience_belief_it_shifts: I need to learn AI / become technical to use it well
lessons.ladders_to_offer: The conversational prompting approach Amir teaches
one_liners: Stop coding your prompts. Start briefing your intern.
Yes, saying 'good job' to ChatGPT actually works. I don't make the rules.
If you can brief an assistant, you can prompt AI.

lessons.lesson: Be willing to offend some people to connect with your core audience
lessons.industry_norm_it_counters: Stay safe, stay broad, don't alienate anyone — appeal to everyone
lessons.the_myth_or_why_norm_is_wrong: Writing for 'everyone' connects with no one. Taking a real stance is what builds genuine connection with the people who matter. If nobody disagrees, nobody deeply agrees either.
lessons.the_aha: The fear of offending the wrong people is what's keeping you invisible to the right ones.
lessons.deeper_truth: Take a stance. Polarize a little. That's what makes you worth following.
lessons.audience_belief_it_shifts: I should keep my content safe and broad
lessons.ladders_to_offer: Amir's opinionated, anti-guru positioning as proof it works
one_liners: If your content offends no one, it moves no one.
Writing for everyone is how you become no one.
A point of view that can't lose followers can't earn fans.

lessons.industry_norm_it_counters: Provide value! Give away the how-to, build trust, and the sale follows.
lessons.the_myth_or_why_norm_is_wrong: If you hand someone the complete how-to, the logical next step is to go do it themselves (which they won't), not to buy from you. 'Value' emails leave people briefly satisfied but not hungry for more.
lessons.the_aha: Giving away the 'how' doesn't build desire — it kills it. Hold the 'how', and the offer becomes the natural next step.
lessons.deeper_truth: Paradigm shifts sell better than how-to. Create the mindset shift; the offer delivers the execution.
lessons.ladders_to_offer: The T.A.L.E. formula and Amir's presell philosophy
one_liners: Giving away the how-to is like giving someone a full meal and trying to sell them dinner.
Value emails satisfy. Paradigm shifts sell.
Teach why it matters. Sell how to do it.

core_lessons.instructions: These are Amir's starter core lessons, drafted from his 'Email Mimic Prompts' guide. Add, edit, or refine over time. To generate a set from scratch (for a new client or a new market), run the core-lessons-creator skill.
